title: Viral AI & Tech Puzzles (Mind-Blowing Reveals)
licenseNote: Starter questions are bundled for local video generation. Buyers can edit, replace, or extend this JSON file.

items.question: Two AI tools answer a legal question. One invents cases; the other requests sources. Which is safer?
items.answer: The one that asks for sources
items.explanation: A confident answer is not proof. Source-grounded uncertainty is safer than fluent hallucination.
items.hint: Read the wording carefully.
choices: The tool with the longer answer
The one that asks for sources
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: A chatbot remembers your first message, then suddenly forgets the document you pasted earlier. Nothing crashed. What invisible limit did you hit?
items.explanation: The model can only keep a limited amount of text in active context.
items.hint: Read the wording carefully.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: A strong password is reused everywhere. One site is breached, then the cloud account is stolen. What failed?
items.explanation: A strong password still fails if the same one is reused on a breached site.
items.hint: Read the wording carefully.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: A team runs the same AI prompt twice. One answer is careful, the next is wildly creative. Which setting probably changed?
items.explanation: Temperature controls randomness and variation in model output.
items.hint: Read the wording carefully.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: Local AI is private but slower; cloud AI is faster but sends data away. What is the tradeoff?

items.question: A developer shares a screenshot containing a secret string. Strangers use the paid account. What leaked?

items.question: An AI summarizes 200 pages but misses a warning on page 187. What went wrong?

items.hint: Look for the unsupervised clustering clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: A payment suddenly differs from a customer's normal pattern. What AI task can flag it?
items.explanation: Anomaly detection identifies observations that deviate strongly from expected patterns.
items.hint: Look for the anomaly detection clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: A model predicts next month's demand from historical sales. What task is this?
items.explanation: Forecasting estimates future values from past patterns and relevant variables.
items.hint: Look for the ai forecasting clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: An AI detector labels human writing as generated. Why should its verdict not be treated as proof?
items.explanation: AI-text detectors can produce false positives and should not be the sole evidence.
items.hint: Look for the ai detection limits clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: Sales rose after an AI tool launched. What must be tested before claiming the AI caused it?
items.explanation: Timing alone shows correlation; experiments or stronger evidence are needed to establish causation.
items.hint: Look for the causal inference clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: A manager accepts an AI recommendation without checking obvious errors. What bias is this?
items.explanation: Automation bias is overreliance on automated output despite reasons to question it.
items.hint: Look for the automation bias clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: A chatbot says 'I feel worried.' Why is treating that sentence as proof of emotion risky?
items.explanation: Human-like language can cause people to attribute feelings or intentions that have not been established.
items.hint: Look for the ai anthropomorphism clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: Training a larger model uses more computation and electricity. What operational concern grows?
items.explanation: More training compute generally requires more electrical energy and supporting infrastructure.
items.hint: Look for the ai energy use clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: A phone analyzes a photo without sending it to the cloud. Where is the AI running?
items.explanation: Edge AI performs inference on or near the device producing the data.
items.hint: Look for the edge ai clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: A large model will not fit on a graphics card. Which hardware resource is exhausted?
items.explanation: Model weights, activations, and caches must fit within available GPU memory.
items.hint: Look for the ai hardware clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?

items.question: A model answers with today's private inventory even though training ended last year. What likely supplied the update?
items.explanation: External retrieval or tool access can provide current data not stored in the model's training weights.
items.hint: Look for the current ai data clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you identify the AI concept before the reveal?
